    1. execute(Object, ConnectionToClient)

Interface Command

All Known Implementing Classes:
AddReservationCommand, AddSpecialDateCommand, AddToWaitingListCommand, AddUserCommand, CancelReservationCommand, CancelWaitingCommand, CreateOpeningHoursCommand, DeleteSpecialDateCommand, DeleteTableCommand, ForgotCodeCommand, GetAllReservationsCommand, GetAllTablesCommand, GetAllUsersCommand, GetAvailableTimesCommand, GetNearestAvailableTimesCommand, GetReportCommand, GetRestaurantSettingsCommand, GetUserInformationCommand, GetUserReservationsCommand, GetWaitingListBetweenDatesCommand, InsertTableCommand, LoginCommand, LogoutCommand, MarkNotifiedCommand, PayCommand, RegisterCommand, RemoveOpeningHoursCommand, SeatCustomerCommand, UpdateClosingTimeCommand, UpdateOpeningTimeCommand, UpdateReservationCommand, UpdateSpecialDateCommand, UpdateTableCommand, UpdateUserCommand
public interface Command
Interface for the Command design pattern used by the server to process client requests. Each implementation of this interface handles a specific action type defined by the communication protocol, allowing for a decoupled and scalable request-handling architecture.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    execute(Object data, ConnectionToClient client)
    Executes the specific logic associated with a client request. * @param data The data payload sent by the client, which may be cast to specific message or entity types depending on the implementation.

  • Method Details

    • execute

      void execute(Object data, ConnectionToClient client)
      Executes the specific logic associated with a client request. * @param data The data payload sent by the client, which may be cast to specific message or entity types depending on the implementation.
      Parameters:
      client - The connection object representing the client that initiated the request, used to send responses back to that specific client.
